commit.message:
fix: correct Dockerfile ARG scope comment and harden Renovate caddy builder customManager

* Initial plan

* fix: correct Dockerfile ARG comment and improve Renovate caddy builder customManager

- Fix inaccurate Dockerfile comment that incorrectly stated ARGs before the
  first FROM cannot reference the ARG in FROM lines. ARGs declared before
  the first FROM ARE available to FROM lines; they are only NOT in scope
  inside build stage bodies (RUN, COPY, etc.).
- Harden the FROM line by hardcoding the version ('2.11.2-builder') instead
  of using the ${CADDY_VERSION} ARG reference. With SHA pinning (@sha256:),
  the ARG reference in the tag was cosmetic only; Docker always pulls by
  digest. Hardcoding enables the Renovate fix below.
- Update Renovate customManager to capture currentValue from the FROM tag
  ('2.11.2-builder') instead of the ARG line. This allows Renovate to:
  - Use extractVersionTemplate to strip '-builder' for semver comparison
  - Look up the manifest for 'caddy:X.Y.Z-builder' (not 'caddy:X.Y.Z')
    to get the correct builder-specific digest
  - Use autoReplaceStringTemplate to atomically update ARG CADDY_VERSION,
    the FROM version tag, and the SHA digest in a single Renovate PR

All other findings from the review were already correctly implemented:
all action SHAs verified, cosign/scout SHA-256 values verified, Alpine 3.23.3
and caddy:2.11.2-builder digests confirmed correct, docker_proxy module name
confirmed, GHCR auth ordering correct, and all other workflow safeguards present.

commit.message:
Fix static delay not accounted for in transport timing (#204)

Follow-up to #194. Per-player `static_delay_ms` wasn't factored into
three transport-level decisions, causing delayed players to have audio
dropped as late, buffer backpressure to undercount headroom, and
late-join targets to land too close to `now`.

Adds `get_static_delay_us()` to the `Role` base class (returns 0 by
default, overridden by `PlayerV1Role`) so `connection.py` and
`push_stream.py` account for the delay without importing player-specific
code. Also adds horizon-based `time_until_end_time_capacity()` on
`BufferTracker` for accurate backpressure when chunk timestamps are
shifted on the timeline.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add nested dictionary binding support (#5738)

### Motivation

Accomplishes
https://github.com/orgs/zauberzeug/projects/6?pane=issue&itemId=82941257
(https://github.com/zauberzeug/nicegui/discussions/2978)

Binding UI elements to nested dictionary keys currently requires
manually managing intermediate
structures. This PR adds first-class support for nested paths using
tuple syntax, e.g.
`ui.input().bind_value(data, ('profile', 'username'))`, making it easy
to bind directly into
nested dicts/objects without boilerplate.

### Implementation

- New `PropertyName = str | tuple[str, ...]` type alias allows property
names to be either
a single string (backward compatible) or a tuple of strings for nested
access
- Helper module `binding_nested.py` provides `_normalize_name`,
`_path_contains_dict` to
  keep the core module clean
- `_get_attribute` / `_set_attribute` / `_has_attribute` now walk nested
paths, with
  `_set_attribute` auto-creating intermediate dicts as needed
- All mixin `bind_*` methods (`bind_value`, `bind_text`,
`bind_visibility`, `bind_enabled`,
  `bind_icon`, `bind_content`) accept the new tuple syntax
- Strict mode error messages distinguish "key" from "nested key" for
clarity
- Internal binding keys normalized to `tuple[str, ...]` for consistent
lookups

### Progress

- [x] I chose a meaningful title that completes the sentence: "If
applied, this PR will..."
- [x] The implementation is complete.
- [x] If this PR addresses a security issue, it has been coordinated via
the [security
advisory](https://github.com/zauberzeug/nicegui/security/advisories/new)
process.
- [x] Pytests have been added (or are not necessary).
- [x] Documentation has been added (docstring update sufficient).
- [x] But do we need a demo, still?

---------

commit.message:
Python: Add header_provider to Streamable HTTP MCP servers (#4849)

* Python: Add header_provider to MCPStreamableHTTPTool (#4808)

Add a header_provider callback parameter to MCPStreamableHTTPTool that
enables injecting dynamic per-request HTTP headers from runtime kwargs
(originating from FunctionInvocationContext.kwargs set in agent middleware).

The implementation uses contextvars and httpx event hooks to ensure headers
are task-local and safe for concurrent tool calls:

- header_provider receives the runtime kwargs dict and returns headers
- call_tool sets a ContextVar before delegating to MCPTool.call_tool
- An httpx request event hook reads from the ContextVar and injects headers

Example usage:
    mcp_tool = MCPStreamableHTTPTool(
        name="web-api",
        url="https://api.example.com/mcp",
        header_provider=lambda kwargs: {
            "X-Auth-Token": kwargs.get("auth_token", ""),
        },
    )

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(fbasim): multi-species community FBA with cross-feeding visualization

- Add S. cerevisiae metabolic model (10 reactions, 9 nodes, fermentation pathway)
- Implement calculateCommunityFlux() with composite S_com matrix coupling
  two strains through shared environmental pool exchange reactions
- Community biomass objective: weighted sum of strain growth rates with
  cross-feeding bonus from metabolite exchange (acetate, ethanol, succinate, lactate)
- Single/Community mode toggle with smooth sidebar transition
- Dual flux map visualization in community mode (E. coli + S. cerevisiae)
- Shared Metabolite Bus with animated directional flow lines
- Glassmorphism containers: Strain A (#DBCDF0), Strain B (#C9E4DE), Pool (#C6DEF1)
  with backdrop-filter blur(10px) and border-radius 24px
- Real-time cross-strain coupling: knocking out genes in Strain A updates
  Strain B growth via exchange flux recalculation
- JetBrains Mono for metrics, Inter for labels, right-aligned flux values
- Add CommunityFBAResult, StrainConfig, ExchangeReaction types

commit.message:
New: [AEA-6292] - Authentication redirect & blocking render improvements (#1865)

## Summary

- :sparkles: New Feature
- :warning: Potential issues that might be caused by this change

### Details

Aims to resolve the bugs identified in:
- AEA-6292
- AEA-6310
- AEA-5831
- AEA-6326

Changes made:
The ensureRoleSelected function has been somewhat re-written to ensure
hierarchical order is correct & that if statements are "looser" fitting
to negative scenarios. Public paths shouldn't be affected by
redirections apart from root and login.

The shouldBlockChildren function has also had additional conditions
added, that will ensure a render block occurs, so that on-page
useEffects don't run.

The shouldBlockChildren now dictates within AccessContext.Provider
return, whether the children or loading page should be shown. This is
believed to resolve an issue where re-rendering wasn't occurring in the
event of a state change if a user was on the loading page in an auth
transition state.

Token lambdas now check for a 'complete' token information in the
DynamoDb table, to prevent the cause of eventual consistency of a
session logout, where a dynamoDb row remains with skeleton information
and no actual credentials.

---------
